content_en: Ilhaam (i>l+l!) - knowledge being put into one's heart; a kind of inspiration or revelation from Allah; mostly emphasized by Sufis Ilm al-kalaam (i>)lSJI i>1c-) - scholastic theology Iltizaam (/Jill) - strict adherence Imaam (i>l..l) - leader in prayer, general leader, scholar Glossary lmaan (u\...;)) - this is "faith," which has its own shareeah definition as explained in the text. lmaan al-Mujmal, al- (ǑI u\...;)'I) - the general or unspecified faith wherein the person has not reached the level of deserving the honorific title of"believer". lnnamaa (W)) a term implying both emphasis and exclusiveness. lnqiyaad ( ..iഊI) - submission, yielding lqaamat al-salaat (•ǒI t..li))- this is the "establishing of the prayer," implying performing it properly according to all of the commands of the shareeah. lraada, al- (•..il.J)'I) - volition lslaam, Islam (!')I...)) - this implies submission, such as submission to Allah; it is also the word for the religion as a whole. lsnaad (..ill...)) - chain of narrators for a hadith lsraaeeliyaat (w4J,i;IY"l) - these are the stories and narrations that have their origin in the books or legends of the Jews and Christians. lstighfaar (.Jlli:i...I) - seeking forgiveness from Allah. lstihsaan (uǓI) - a method of exercising personal opinion in order to avoid any rigidity and unfairness that might result from the literal enforcement of the law; juristic preference. lstiqaamah, al- (t..W..YI) - remaining steadfast along the straight fast. ltikaaj(wǔI) - to seclude oneself in the mosque for the purpose of worship. lthm (Ǖ!) - sin Jaahiliyyah, al- (Î4JI) - the period or time of ignorance; in particular, it refers to the time before the coming of the Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him), however it can be used for any time that shares the same essential characteristics of ignorance or negligence of God's laws. Jaaizah (•Jiǖ) - with respect to one's visiting guest, it is where the guest should be given the best type of food on the first day and night and then after that he eats what the host would usually eat. Jahmites - a common Western term for the Jahmiyyah Jahmiyyah, al- (ǗI) - the followers of Jahm ibn Safwaan, who was killed for his beliefs by the Muslim ruler. He virtually denied all of Allah's attributes. He claimed that imaan is simply a matter of having knowledge that Allah exists. Jibreel (J.!..J:1;i.) - the Angel Gabriel Jihad ( ..iഈ) - to exert oneself, strive, struggle; in particular in fiqh, it refers to fighting against the unbelievers in order to spread the word of Allah. Jizyah ("-:i.») - this is the tax that non-Muslim citizens of the Islamic state must pay in lieu of military service. Kaabah (ഇ) - the House of Allah in Makkah to which the Hajj is made. Kaafir (.)\S) - a disbeliever Karramites - common Western term for the Karraamiyyah Karramiyyah, al- (Ifill) - This heretical group, which have very little influence in the history oflslam, claimed that imaan is simply a statement of the tongue. Kash/(ǘ) - "spiritual unveiling," where truths are mystically revealed to one's heart; mostly emphasized by Sufis Kataba (ഉ)- literally, this means "he recorded," however it is often used in the Quran and hadith to mean, in reference to Allah, "He ordered, prescribed." Commentary on the Forty Hadith of al-Nawawi Khaleefah (ୃ) - the leader of the Muslim nation, often referred to as "caliph" in English Khaleel (J..h) - beloved and intimate friend Khalq, al- (ǰI) - the creation Kharijites - a common Western term for the Khawaarij Khata (Uu) - mistake Khawaarij, al- (e;:)ƾI) - one of the fust heretical groups in the history oflslam. Known for their extremist views, they even declared the caliphs Uthman and Ali disbelievers. They believed that anyone who commits a major sin falls out of the fold of Islam. The Prophet (peace be upon him) prophesized their coming and stated that they should be fought. Khuluq, al- (ǰI) - behaviors, manners Khushu' (t#) - lowering, humbling and submitting oneself Khutbah (ǯ) - address, speech; in particular, the speech given by the Imam at the Friday Prayer is called a khutbah Khutbah al-haajah (4\..:JI ǯ) - these are the words with which the Prophet (peace be upon him) would commonly begin his speeches. Kibr (fa.) - pride, arrogance Kufr (fa) - disbelief, the opposite of imaan. Kunya (A..;.;S) - this is a common name by which people are referred; it is of the form, "father of," or "mother of," such as, "father of Muhammad." Kurbah (ƭ.fi) - anything which distresses the soul La ilaaha illa-llah (..lil Y! ..Uj Y) - "There is none worthy of worship except Allah" Lai/at al-Qadar (..JƿI ആ) - "the Night of Decree" Maasiyah (ǀ) - a sinful act Maaturidiyyah, al- (ǁ.l;!_jiWI) - The followers of Abu Mansur al-Maturidi (d. 333 A.H.). This group is very close to the Ashaariyyah in their beliefs. madhab ( ->"11) - the esophagus Marfoo (t.,! .>" )- this is a report or statement that is traced all the way back to the Prophet (peace be upon him).
content_id: Ilham — ilmu yang dimasukkan ke dalam hati seseorang; semacam inspirasi atau wahyu dari Allah; kebanyakan ditekankan oleh kaum Sufi. Ilm al-Kalam — teologi skolastik. Iltizam — kepatuhan yang ketat. Imam — pemimpin dalam salat, pemimpin umum, ulama. Iman — ini adalah 'keyakinan', yang memiliki definisi syariat tersendiri sebagaimana dijelaskan dalam teks. Iman al-Mujmal — keyakinan yang umum atau tidak spesifik di mana seseorang belum mencapai tingkat yang layak mendapatkan gelar kehormatan 'orang beriman'. Innama — istilah yang mengandung makna penekanan sekaligus pengkhususan. Inqiyad — penyerahan diri, kepatuhan. Iqamat al-Shalat — 'menegakkan salat', yang berarti melaksanakannya dengan benar sesuai dengan semua perintah syariat. Al-Iradah — kehendak. Islam — ini mengandung makna penyerahan diri, seperti penyerahan diri kepada Allah; ini juga adalah kata untuk agama secara keseluruhan. Isnad — rantai perawi untuk sebuah hadis. Isra'iliyyat — kisah-kisah dan riwayat-riwayat yang bersumber dari kitab-kitab atau legenda-legenda orang Yahudi dan Kristen. Istighfar — memohon ampunan kepada Allah. Istihsan — metode penggunaan pendapat pribadi untuk menghindari kekakuan dan ketidakadilan yang mungkin timbul dari penerapan hukum secara literal; preferensi yurisprudensial. Al-Istiqamah — tetap teguh di atas jalan yang lurus. I'tikaf — mengurung diri di masjid untuk tujuan ibadah. Itsm — dosa. Al-Jahiliyyah — masa atau periode kebodohan; secara khusus, merujuk pada masa sebelum datangnya Nabi Muhammad ﷺ, namun dapat digunakan untuk masa kapan pun yang memiliki karakteristik kebodohan atau pengabaian hukum-hukum Allah yang sama. Ja'izah — berkenaan dengan tamu yang berkunjung, ini adalah di mana tamu harus diberikan makanan terbaik pada hari dan malam pertama, kemudian setelah itu dia makan apa yang biasa dimakan tuan rumah. Jahmiyyah — para pengikut Jahm ibn Shafwan, yang dibunuh karena keyakinannya oleh penguasa Muslim. Beliau pada dasarnya mengingkari semua sifat-sifat Allah dan mengklaim bahwa iman hanyalah soal memiliki pengetahuan bahwa Allah ada. Jibril — Malaikat Jibril. Jihad — bersungguh-sungguh, berjuang; secara khusus dalam fiqih, merujuk pada berperang melawan orang-orang kafir untuk menyebarkan kalimat Allah. Jizyah — ini adalah pajak yang harus dibayar oleh warga negara non-Muslim di negara Islam sebagai pengganti wajib militer. Ka'bah — Rumah Allah di Makkah yang menjadi tujuan ibadah haji. Kafir — orang yang tidak beriman. Karramiyyah — kelompok sesat ini, yang sangat sedikit pengaruhnya dalam sejarah Islam, mengklaim bahwa iman hanyalah pernyataan lisan. Kasyf — 'penyingkapan spiritual', di mana kebenaran-kebenaran secara mistis terungkap kepada hati seseorang; kebanyakan ditekankan oleh kaum Sufi. Kataba — secara harfiah berarti 'dia mencatat', namun sering digunakan dalam Al-Quran dan hadis untuk merujuk kepada Allah, yang berarti 'Dia memerintahkan, mewajibkan'. Khalifah — pemimpin umat Islam, sering disebut 'caliph' dalam bahasa Inggris. Khalil — kekasih dan teman yang sangat akrab. Al-Khalq — ciptaan. Al-Khawarij — salah satu kelompok sesat pertama dalam sejarah Islam. Dikenal karena pandangan ekstremis mereka, mereka bahkan mengkafirkan para khalifah Utsman dan Ali. Mereka berpendapat bahwa siapa pun yang melakukan dosa besar telah keluar dari Islam. Nabi ﷺ telah meramalkan kemunculan mereka dan menyatakan bahwa mereka harus diperangi. Al-Khuluq — perilaku, akhlak. Khusyu' — merendahkan, menghinakan, dan menyerahkan diri. Khutbah — pidato, ceramah; secara khusus, ceramah yang disampaikan oleh imam pada Salat Jumat disebut khutbah. Khutbah al-Hajah — kata-kata pembuka yang biasa digunakan Nabi ﷺ untuk memulai pidato-pidatonya. Kibr — kesombongan, keangkuhan. Kufr — kekufuran, lawan dari iman. Kunya — nama julukan yang umum dipakai oleh orang-orang; berbentuk 'ayah dari' atau 'ibu dari', seperti 'ayah Muhammad'. Kurbah — segala sesuatu yang menyusahkan jiwa. La ilaha illallah — 'Tidak ada yang berhak disembah kecuali Allah'. Lailat al-Qadar — 'Malam Kemuliaan'. Ma'siyah — perbuatan dosa. Al-Maturidiyyah — para pengikut Abu Mansur al-Maturidi (w. 333 H). Kelompok ini sangat dekat dengan Asy'ariyyah dalam keyakinan-keyakinan mereka. Mazhab — mazhab fiqih. Marfu' — ini adalah laporan atau pernyataan yang ditelusuri kembali hingga ke Nabi ﷺ.
content_ar: Ilhaam (i>l+l!) - العلم being put into one's القلب; a kind of الوحي or الوحي from الله; mostly emphasized by Sufis Ilm al-kalaam (i>)lSJI i>1c-) - scholastic theology Iltizaam (/Jill) - strict adherence Imaam (i>l..l) - leader in الصلاة, general leader, scholar Glossary lmaan (u\...;)) - this is "الإيمان," which has its own shareeah definition as explained in the text. lmaan al-Mujmal, al- (ǑI u\...;)'I) - the general or unspecified الإيمان wherein the person has not reached the level of deserving the honorific title of"المؤمن". lnnamaa (W)) a term implying both emphasis and exclusiveness. lnqiyaad ( ..iഊI) - submission, yielding lqaamat al-salaat (•ǒI t..li))- this is the "establishing of the الصلاة," implying performing it properly وفقاً لـ all of the commands of the shareeah. lraada, al- (•..il.J)'I) - volition lslaam, الإسلام (!')I...)) - this implies submission, مثل submission to الله; it is أيضاً the word for the religion as a whole. lsnaad (..ill...)) - chain of narrators for a الحديث lsraaeeliyaat (w4J,i;IY"l) - these are the stories and narrations that have their origin in the الكتب or legends of the Jews and Christians. lstighfaar (.Jlli:i...I) - seeking المغفرة from الله. lstihsaan (uǓI) - a method of exercising personal opinion من أجل avoid any rigidity and unfairness that might result from the literal enforcement of the law; juristic preference. lstiqaamah, al- (t..W..YI) - remaining steadfast along the straight fast. ltikaaj(wǔI) - to seclude oneself in the المسجد for the purpose of worship. lthm (Ǖ!) - الذنب Jaahiliyyah, al- (Î4JI) - the period or time of ignorance; وبالأخص, it refers to the time قبل the coming of النبي ﷺ محمد ﷺ (ﷺ), غير أن it can be used for any time that shares the same الجوهري characteristics of ignorance or negligence of God's laws. Jaaizah (•Jiǖ) - with الاحترام to one's visiting guest, it is where the guest should be given the best type of food on the أولاً day and night and then بعد that he eats what the host would usually eat. Jahmites - a common Western term for the Jahmiyyah Jahmiyyah, al- (ǗI) - the followers of Jahm ibn Safwaan, who was killed for his beliefs by the مسلم ruler. He virtually denied all of الله's attributes. He claimed that imaan is simply a matter of having العلم that الله exists. Jibreel (J.!..J:1;i.) - the Angel جبريل ﷺ Jihad ( ..iഈ) - to exert oneself, strive, struggle; وبالأخص in الفقه, it refers to fighting against the unbelievers من أجل spread the word of الله. Jizyah ("-:i.») - this is the tax that non-مسلم citizens of the Islamic state must pay in lieu of military service. Kaabah (ഇ) - the House of الله in مكة المكرمة to which the الحج is made. Kaafir (.)\S) - a disbeliever Karramites - common Western term for the Karraamiyyah Karramiyyah, al- (Ifill) - This heretical group, which have very little influence in the history oflslam, claimed that imaan is simply a statement of the tongue.
